stub: She is a Conservative MP who comes from a white working class background.
constituency: She is from a constituency that is not your constituency.
personalization: She rarely speaks out and votes against her party leadership.
substantive: She opposes adopting more stringent measures to limit immigration.
justification: In political debates, she regularly uses arguments that appeal to people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background).
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “In a democracy, the voter has the last word. That is why I always listen and respond to what voters want.”
text: She is a Conservative MP who comes from a white working class background. She is from a constituency that is not your constituency. She rarely speaks out and votes against her party leadership. She opposes adopting more stringent measures to limit immigration. In political debates, she regularly uses arguments that appeal to people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background). Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “In a democracy, the voter has the last word. That is why I always listen and respond to what voters want.”
